samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <reg-enum id="state" description="Current state of the FSM">ZERO</reg-enum>
            <reg-enum id="next_state" description="Next state of the FSM">ZERO</reg-enum>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On each positive edge of the clock signal clk, the current state of the FSM is updated. 
            If the reset signal rst_n is low, the state is set to ZERO. 
            Otherwise, the next state is loaded. 
            In state ZERO, if the input signal a is high, the next state is set to ONE. 
            In state ONE, if the input signal a is low, the state is set back to ZERO. 
        </sequential>
        The rising edge of the input signal a is detected when the state changes from ZERO to ONE. 
        <sequential>
            If the input signal a is high when the state is ZERO, the output signal rise is set to high on the next positive edge of the clock. 
            If the input signal a is low, the state is set back to ZERO, and the output signal rise is set to low.
            Similarly, the falling edge of the input signal a is detected when the state changes from ONE to ZERO. 
            If the input signal a is low when the state is ONE, the output signal down is set to high on the next positive edge of the clock. 
            If the input signal a is high, the state is set back to ZERO, and the output signal down is set to low.
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>
